rejection_reason: The statement is vague and aspirational: it lacks quantifiable targets (no % pollutant reduction, flow rates, or BMP counts), no timeline or deadline, no binding mechanism (law/regulation/permit cited), no spatial specificity (no watershed/jurisdiction), and no defined technological shift. policy_statement: Increase the city's share of renewable energy and reduce energy consumption in line with the state's renewable portfolio standard.
verbatim_text: The City has been known for its efforts in renewable energy and energy efficiency. This plan will enable both municipal operations and the community to increase its overall share of renewable energy in line with the state's renewable portfolio standard while reducing energy consumption.
rejection_reason:
- Lacks quantifiable targets: no percentages, baselines, units, or numeric goals (“increase”/“reduce” without metrics).
- No timeline or deadline to assess progress.
- No binding mechanism: only “in line with the state’s renewable portfolio standard” (an external benchmark) without adopting it via ordinance/regulation.
- Spatial specificity not established in the policy as provided.
- Language is aspirational/process-oriented (“plan will enable,” “increase,” “reduce”) without a concrete roadmap.
- While it signals a directional shift (more renewables, lower consumption), it is not actionable/measurable as written.
weak_signals: enable, in line with, increase, reduce, overall share, efforts, plan will enable
strong_signals: reference to state renewable portfolio standard (external benchmark), directional intent to increase renewable energy, directional intent to reduce energy consumption, mentions municipal operations and community scope

policy_statement: Adopt STAR/LEED objectives including Environmental Justice, Active Living, Community Health and Systems, Food Access and Nutrition, and Indoor Air Quality.
verbatim_text:
STAR/LEED objectives include:

- Environmental Justice: Reduce polluted and toxic environments
- Active Living: Enable adults and kids to maintain healthy, active lifestyles
- Community  Health  and  Systems: Achieve  positive  health outcomes  and  minimize  health  risks
- Food Access and Nutrition: Ensure access to fresh, healthful food
- Indoor Air Quality: Ensure healthy indoor air quality
rejection_reason: The policy lists broad aspirations (reduce, enable, achieve, ensure) without quantifiable targets, baselines, metrics, or units; there is no timeline/deadline; no binding/legal mechanism is indicated; and no spatial scope is defined. While it references recognized frameworks (STAR/LEED), it does not specify a certification level, score, or compliance requirement that would make it measurable or enforceable. As written, it is largely aspirational and process-oriented, lacking actionable specifics in any strong dimension.
weak_signals: "adopt objectives"; "reduce"; "enable"; "achieve positive health outcomes"; "ensure access"; "ensure healthy indoor air quality"; thematic labels without metrics (Environmental Justice, Active Living); framework name-dropping (STAR/LEED) without level or criteria
strong_signals: Reference to established standards (STAR/LEED); identifies thematic focus areas (Indoor Air Quality, Food Access)
